name: Synovus Bank
description_short: Synovus Bank is an FDIC-insured mortgage lender with 130+ years of history and ~$116B in assets, operating nearly 400 branches across nine states after merging with Pinnacle Financial Partners.
description_long:
Synovus Bank has operated for more than 130 years as a trusted financial institution, building relationships and local decision-making practices across communities it serves. The company recently joined with Pinnacle Financial Partners to form one of the nation's leading banks, consolidating approximately $116 billion in assets and expanding its presence through nearly 400 branch locations, more than 500 ATMs, and operations spanning nine states and the District of Columbia.

Synovus specializes in mortgage lending and offers comprehensive banking services including personal and business financial solutions. Their mortgage services are complemented by digital banking through My Synovus, a secure website and mobile app platform that enables customers to manage finances, pay bills, and set financial alerts. They also provide the Synovus Inspire premium banking relationship program with exclusive benefits and a Personal Resource Center offering 130+ years of accumulated financial knowledge.

The company distinguishes itself through its commitment to personal service and local decision-making despite its large scale. Each Sarasota-area location maintains consistent weekday hours (9 AM–5 PM Monday through Friday) with dedicated mortgage specialists available. As part of the transition to Pinnacle Financial Partners through 2027, Synovus emphasizes continuity and stability while modernizing its digital offerings. The bank is FDIC-insured, backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government.

However, potential borrowers should note that Synovus is in the midst of a major institutional transition and name change through 2027, which may create operational uncertainties. The website provides limited specific information about mortgage products, rates, terms, or qualification requirements—customers must contact the bank directly for detailed loan options. As a large regional bank rather than a mortgage-specialized lender, borrowers seeking competitive rates or specialized loan products may need to compare with dedicated mortgage companies.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: FDIC-insured with backing of full faith and credit of U.S. Government
130+ years of banking history and established customer relationships
~$116 billion in assets with nearly 400 branches and 500+ ATMs across nine states
Digital banking platform (My Synovus) with mobile app for 24/7 account management
Synovus Inspire premium banking program with exclusive benefits for qualifying customers
Local decision-making and personal service model across all branches
Personal Resource Center offering free financial education and guidance
Multiple Sarasota-area locations with consistent business hours (9 AM–5 PM weekdays)
cons: Currently undergoing major institutional transition and name change to Pinnacle Financial Partners through 2027, creating potential service disruptions
Website provides no specific information about mortgage products, rates, terms, APRs, or qualification requirements
Limited online mortgage application or rate quote tools visible on the site—requires phone contact or in-person visit
As a regional bank, may not offer specialized mortgage products or competitive rates compared to dedicated mortgage lenders
Lobby hours end at 5 PM weekdays and closed weekends, limiting accessibility for working customers
best_for: Existing Synovus/Pinnacle customers seeking seamless mortgage services from their current bank
Borrowers in Florida or other Synovus service areas (nine states plus D.C.) who value local decision-making
Customers seeking FDIC-insured mortgages with established institutional stability and 130+ year history
Buyers who prioritize comprehensive banking relationships and access to premium Synovus Inspire benefits
diagnosis: Synovus Bank is best for customers seeking mortgage services from an established, FDIC-insured regional bank with strong local presence and comprehensive banking relationships. The primary caveat is the ongoing institutional transition through 2027 and the lack of transparent mortgage product/rate information online—borrowers must contact the bank directly to compare specific loan options and terms.
services: My Synovus digital banking platform with website and mobile app
Bill payment and financial management tools
Synovus Inspire premium banking relationship program
In-person banking at 1605 Main Street and nearby branch locations
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
